texto: I have more of a relationship with the subject than I do with my camera equipment. To me, camera equipment is like a tin of shoe polish and a brush - I use that as a tool, but my basic camera is my emotion and my eyes. It's not anything to do with the wonderful cameras I use.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Don McCullin: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “I have more of a relationship with the subject than I do with my camera equipment. To me, camera …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, eyes, me pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de me e camera.

frase_original: I have more of a relationship with the subject than I do with my camera equipment. To me, camera equipment is like a tin of shoe polish and a brush - I use that as a tool, but my basic camera is my emotion and my eyes. It's not anything to do with the wonderful cameras I use.
